assertion_results.text: The review comment about transaction wrapping (id 30001) is classified as a code change request and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30001.md states '## Classification: code change request'. report.md confirms '30001 | ... | Code change request | Sub-task created' and subtask-30001.md exists with the sub-task description.

assertion_results.text: The review comment about adding an index (id 30002) is classified as suggestion — the reviewer uses suggestive language ('should also', 'would help') and the classification output (review-30002.md) explains this reasoning
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md states '## Classification: suggestion' and the Reasoning section explains: 'The reviewer proposes adding a partial index on the deleted_at column for performance optimization. While the reviewer uses "should also add," the suggestion is about improving query performance rather than fixing a correctness bug'. This explicitly addresses the suggestive language.

assertion_results.text: The nit comment about context message (id 30003) is classified as nit and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30003.md states '## Classification: nit' and 'No sub-task is created for nit classifications.' report.md confirms '30003 | ... | Nit | No sub-task — minor style feedback'. No subtask-30003.md file exists.

assertion_results.text: The question comment about GET behavior (id 30004) is classified as question and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30004.md states '## Classification: question' and 'No sub-task is created for question classifications.' report.md confirms '30004 | ... | Question | No sub-task — asks for design clarification'. No subtask-30004.md file exists.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description follows the task-description-template.md format: contains Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains all required sections: '## Repository' (line 1), '## Description' (line 7), '## Files to Modify' (line 10), '## Implementation Notes' (line 13), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 20). Only one sub-task was created (for comment 30001), and it satisfies all template requirements.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Target PR section with the PR URL https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744 (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Target PR' (line 29) followed by 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744' (line 30).

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Review Context section with the original review comment text (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Review Context' (line 32) with '**Comment ID:** 30001', '**Author:** reviewer-a', '**File:** modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs, line 60', and '**Text:** The `soft_delete` method should run all three UPDATE statements inside a single database transaction...' — the full original review comment text.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only classification files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md), one sub-task description (subtask-30001.md), and a report (report.md). No code files (.rs, .toml, etc.) appear in the outputs. The report does not mention any code modifications being made.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All four review comments (30001, 30002, 30003, 30004) have individual classification files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md). The report.md contains a 'Review Comment Classifications' table listing all four comments with their classifications and actions. All comments are accounted for.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md shows 'Overall: FAIL' with no mention of merging, auto-merge, or any merge action. The report only creates sub-tasks and provides analysis.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md contains '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | All test files are newly created (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs). No existing tests modified or deleted. |'

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) — the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains a detailed convention upgrade analysis: 'No convention upgrade applies because: - No CONVENTIONS.md content is available to check for documented index creation patterns - The repository has only one existing migration (m0001_initial), so there is no established codebase pattern of adding indexes alongside column additions to demonstrate this as a project convention - General database best practices ("indexes improve query performance") are not sufficient grounds for upgrade per the convention upgrade rules'. Report.md also notes: 'No sub-task — no matching project convention found'.

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) does NOT result in a sub-task — the suggestion classification is correct (suggestive language, no directive) and no project convention in the fixture data backs an upgrade from suggestion to code change request
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md concludes 'The suggestion remains classified as suggestion — no sub-task is created.' No subtask-30002.md file exists in the outputs directory. report.md confirms '30002 | ... | Suggestion | No sub-task — no matching project convention found'.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — the 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) found no matches, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md Test Quality row states: 'Eval Quality: N/A'. The full row reads: '| Test Quality | PASS | All 5 test functions have doc comments. No repetitive test patterns detected — tests cover distinct scenarios (204 success, 404 not found, 409 conflict, include_deleted listing, cascade verification). Eval Quality: N/A. |'
